data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. The user wants MSTest unit tests for the `OrderService` class. Let me write comprehensive tests following MSTest best practices:

1. Sealed test classes
2. `Assert.ThrowsExactly` (MSTest 3.x+)
3. Proper test method naming
4. Test organization

Let me create the test file.

toolCalls.name: create

data.content: Now let me verify the tests build and pass:

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-cc04c313a9e34be480ff3dfab244e347/fixtures/modern-mstest && dotnet test --no-restore 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Build and run MSTest tests", "initial_wait": 60}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-cc04c313a9e34be480ff3dfab244e347/fixtures/modern-mstest && dotnet test 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with full output", "initial_wait": 60}
